item.title: Real-world Experience of Upadacitinib Reinduction and High-dose Maintenance Therapy in Inflammatory Bowel Disease.
item.description: Clinical gastroenterology and hepatology : the official clinical practice journal of the American Gastroenterological Association - Upadacitinib (UPA) is approved for the treatment of moderately to severely active Crohn's disease (CD) or ulcerative colitis (UC). Responders to UPA 45 mg daily (QD) may lose response (LOR) after t...

item.title: Upadacitinib Maintenance Therapy in Crohn's Disease: Final Results From the Randomized Phase 3 U-ENDURE Study.
item.description: Clinical gastroenterology and hepatology : the official clinical practice journal of the American Gastroenterological Association - RCT - Upadacitinib, an oral, reversible Janus kinase inhibitor, demonstrated efficacy and safety in patients with Crohn's disease in 2 phase III induction trials (U-EXCEL and U-EXCEED) and in a primary a...

item.title: Comparative Effectiveness of Tofacitinib vs Upadacitinib for the Treatment of Acute Severe Ulcerative Colitis.
item.description: Clinical gastroenterology and hepatology : the official clinical practice journal of the American Gastroenterological Association - Multicenter study - Tofacitinib and upadacitinib are Janus kinase (JAK) inhibitors that are increasingly used for the treatment of acute severe ulcerative colitis (ASUC). However, comparative analyses of safety and ef...

item.title: Birth Outcomes in Women Who Have Taken Vedolizumab in Pregnancy: Results From the Vedolizumab Pregnancy Exposure Registry.
item.description: The American journal of gastroenterology - Observational study - There are limited data on the safety of vedolizumab in pregnancy for the treatment of Crohn's disease or ulcerative colitis. Between 2015 and 2022, the Organization of Teratology Information Specia...
